senses.definition: (of a person) interested in learning and knowing about things

examples.x: She's so curious; she always asks questions and loves to explore new topics.
His curious nature led him to read books on a wide range of subjects.
The curious student excels in school because of their genuine interest in learning.
The curious traveler immerses themselves in local culture and customs wherever they go.
She was always curious about different cultures and loved traveling to new places.

senses.definition: having no fear when doing dangerous or painful things

examples.x: Despite the danger, he remained brave and rescued the injured hiker from the mountain.
The brave firefighter rushed into the burning building to save lives.
Despite the stormy weather, the brave sailor set out to rescue the stranded boaters.
The brave explorer ventured into the uncharted jungle, undeterred by the risks.
The brave doctor performed the risky surgery with steady hands, saving the patient's life.

senses.definition: showing a lack of seriousness, often in a playful way

examples.x: She was being silly when she wore mismatched shoes on purpose.
The kids were being silly, running around the playground and laughing.
She felt silly when she tripped over nothing in front of her friends.

senses.definition: feeling satisfied with someone or one's possessions, achievements, etc.
possessing enough skill or knowledge in a certain field or job

examples.x: She is an experienced teacher with over twenty years of classroom experience.
His experienced leadership guided the team through challenging projects with confidence.
The experienced doctor accurately diagnosed the patient's condition based on years of medical practice.
With experienced hands, the carpenter crafted intricate furniture pieces with precision.
The experienced traveler knows how to navigate foreign countries and cultures with ease.

senses.definition: feeling confident and optimistic and thinking about the bright side of a situation

examples.x: Despite facing setbacks, she maintained a positive attitude, believing that things would improve with time.
He approached every new challenge with a positive outlook, seeing it as an opportunity for growth.
The team remained positive in the face of adversity, rallying together to find solutions.
His positive energy was contagious, lifting the spirits of those around him.
They faced the competition with a positive mindset, believing in their ability to succeed.

senses.definition: never considering the good qualities of someone or something and is often quick to lose hope

examples.x: Her negative personality makes it difficult to see the bright side of any situation.
He tends to be a negative person and not give new ideas a fair chance.
It's important to avoid surrounding yourself with negative people that bring you down.
His negative persona stemmed from past disappointments and failures.

senses.definition: always putting one's interests first and not caring about the needs or rights of others

examples.x: She's so selfish; she never considers how her actions affect others.
His selfish behavior caused resentment among his friends and family.
The selfish driver cut off other cars in traffic without a second thought.
The selfish coworker always takes credit for other people's work.
The selfish politician prioritized their own agenda over the needs of their constituents.

senses.definition: feeling very unhappy or uncomfortable

examples.x: She felt miserable after failing the exam.
He caught the flu and spent the week feeling miserable in bed.
Working long hours without a break made her feel miserable.
He looked miserable sitting alone in the corner.
The team had a miserable performance during the final match.

senses.definition: possessing a natural skill or ability for something

examples.x: She is a talented dancer, known for her grace and precision on stage.
His talented hands create beautiful pottery that is admired by many.
The team has several talented players this season.
The talented writer crafts engaging stories that captivate readers from beginning to end.
The company is looking for talented engineers to join their team.

senses.definition: able to remain calm, especially in challenging or difficult situations, without becoming annoyed or anxious

examples.x: Despite the long wait, she remained patient while waiting for her turn.
The doctor remained patient with the elderly patient who asked repeated questions about their medication.
Despite the delays, she remained patient during her commute, knowing that traffic was out of her control.
The teacher was patient with the struggling student, providing extra help until they understood the concept.

senses.definition: having the ability to learn or understand quickly

examples.x: The keen student quickly understood the complex mathematical problem.
The keen scientist eagerly absorbed new research findings in their field.
With a keen mind for languages, he became fluent in Spanish after just a few months of study.
The keen apprentice absorbed the techniques of the trade with remarkable speed.

senses.definition: telling the truth and having no intention of cheating or stealing

examples.x: The honest cashier returned the extra change that the customer had mistakenly received.
Despite the temptation, he remained honest and refused to take credit for someone else's work.
The honest mechanic provided a fair assessment of the car's condition, even though it meant less profit for the garage.
The honest witness testified in court, providing crucial evidence for the case.
Even in difficult situations, she remained honest and transparent, refusing to compromise her principles.

senses.definition: having a desire to physically or mentally harm someone

examples.x: The cruel bully tormented his classmates daily, enjoying their distress.
She showed her cruel nature by neglecting her pet's basic needs.
The cruel dictator oppressed his people, showing no mercy to those who opposed him.
She couldn't believe her sister's cruel betrayal, revealing her secrets to everyone.
The cruel treatment of animals at the factory farm outraged animal rights activists.

senses.definition: causing slight anger

examples.x: The annoying sound of construction outside disrupted her concentration.
The annoying habit of tapping his foot constantly during the meeting distracted everyone.
Dealing with annoying telemarketing calls during dinner became a regular annoyance.
The annoying delay in public transportation made commuters late for work.
The annoying buzzing of mosquitoes kept them awake all night.

senses.definition: lacking confidence and needing to be emotionally supported a lot

examples.x: She's feeling needy lately, constantly seeking validation from her friends.
The needy child clung to his mother's side, afraid to explore on his own.
He became increasingly needy after the breakup, seeking comfort from anyone who would listen.
The needy employee constantly sought approval and praise from their supervisor.
The needy friend relied heavily on others for advice and guidance in making decisions.

senses.definition: unwilling to change one's attitude or opinion despite good reasons to do so

examples.x: Despite overwhelming evidence, he remained stubborn in his belief that he was always right.
Her stubborn refusal to compromise led to frequent arguments with her colleagues.
The stubborn child refused to eat their vegetables, even after being told they were good for them.
His stubborn insistence on doing things his own way often caused delays in the project.
Despite multiple attempts to convince him otherwise, he remained stubborn in his decision to quit his job.

senses.definition: having an appealing quality

examples.x: Her sense of style was so cool that everyone wanted to emulate her.
The band’s latest album has a cool vibe that resonates with fans.
He walked into the room with a cool confidence that drew everyone’s attention.
The café had a cool atmosphere, perfect for hanging out with friends.
They designed the new logo to have a cool, modern look that appeals to younger customers.

senses.definition: able to do things as one wants without needing help from others

examples.x: She's an independent woman, capable of making her own decisions and taking care of herself.
Despite her young age, she's quite independent, managing her own finances and responsibilities.
He's known for his independent spirit, preferring to work alone rather than in a team.
The independent artist pursued her passion without seeking validation from others.
The independent thinker challenges conventional wisdom and forges her own path in life.

senses.definition: trying or wishing to gain great success, power, or wealth

examples.x: Always the ambitious student, she dreamed of attending a top university and then establishing her own global enterprise.
She is an ambitious entrepreneur, constantly seeking new opportunities to expand her business empire.
Maria is an ambitious student, always aiming for top grades and participating in numerous extracurricular activities.
The ambitious politician outlined bold plans for economic reform to drive the country's growth and prosperity.
His ambitious nature led him to take on challenging projects that others deemed impossible, proving his capabilities time and again.

senses.definition: displaying friendliness, kindness, or enthusiasm

examples.x: She greeted us with a warm smile and made us feel right at home.
He has a warm personality; he's always ready to listen and offer support.
The warm welcome we received from the staff made our visit to the hotel memorable.
His warm gestures of kindness, such as offering to help, endeared him to his coworkers.
The community's warm response to the charity event exceeded expectations.

senses.definition: showing warmth and friendliness to a guest or visitor

examples.x: The hotel staff provided a welcoming reception for arriving guests, making them feel at home.
Despite being strangers, they were met with a welcoming attitude by the locals in the small town.

senses.definition: possessing a friendly personality and willing to spend time with people

examples.x: Jane is very sociable and enjoys engaging with large groups of people at parties and social events.
Introverts may find large gatherings draining, whereas more sociable personalities tend to recharge from time spent in groups.
As a very sociable person, John never had trouble meeting new people when traveling alone due to his friendly nature.
She's always been a sociable person, making friends wherever she goes.
The new employee seemed sociable, chatting with coworkers during lunch.

senses.definition: having a willingness to freely give or share something with others, without expecting anything in return

examples.x: She's a generous donor, always contributing to charitable causes and helping those in need.
Despite facing financial struggles, he remained generous, sharing what little he had with others who were less fortunate.
The host was incredibly generous, offering us plenty of food and drinks.
The generous tip left by the satisfied customer reflected their appreciation for the exceptional service they received.
They thanked her for the generous offer to pay for the repairs.

senses.definition: showing kindness and empathy toward others

examples.x: He has a gentle nature, always treating others with kindness and understanding.
The gentle caregiver provides loving support and comfort to those in need.
The gentle teacher encourages students with kindness and encouragement.
Even in difficult times, he maintains a gentle demeanor, offering solace to those around him.
The gentle nature of the horse made it easy to ride.

senses.definition: not judging someone and forgiving toward them when they do something wrong or make a mistake
very good at doing something particular

examples.x: She is a skillful surgeon, known for her steady hands and precise techniques in the operating room.
His skillful handling of the situation defused the tension and brought about a peaceful resolution.
The skillful artist creates intricate paintings that showcase their mastery of color and form.
With skillful maneuvering, the pilot safely landed the plane during adverse weather conditions.
The skillful dancer moves with grace and fluidity, captivating the audience with their performance.

senses.definition: (of a person) unwilling to become involved in a dispute or anything violent

examples.x: She's a peaceful individual who always seeks to resolve conflicts through dialogue and understanding.
The peaceful protester stood silently, holding a sign advocating for unity and justice.
Despite the provocation, she remained peaceful and composed, refusing to escalate the situation.
Despite the chaos around him, he remained peaceful and centered, spreading calmness to those around him.
The peaceful leader promoted reconciliation and unity, guiding the community towards a peaceful future.

senses.definition: (of a person) uncertain or hesitant about something

examples.x: He appeared doubtful about the reliability of the information he received.
She seemed doubtful about the decision, unsure if it was the right choice.
He was doubtful about the success of the project, considering the tight deadline.
She felt doubtful about the sincerity of his apology, wondering if it was genuine.
The student looked doubtful when asked if he understood the complex math problem.

senses.definition: a person who likes to threaten, scare, or hurt others, particularly people who are weaker

examples.x: It's important for parents to teach their children about empathy and kindness to prevent them from becoming bullies.
She stood up to the bully and reported their behavior to the school authorities.
Teachers must address cases of bullying by identifying the bully.
The school counselor offered support and guidance to students who had been targeted by bullies.
The bully was given a warning for his behavior.
